channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The Rise of AI Inference Chips: Transforming Efficiency
item.description: AI inference chips are designed for efficient artificial intelligence solutions, transforming the way we use technology.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Elon Musk’s DOGE agency may launch a mobile app to file your taxes
item.description: Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) doesn’t have a staff yet and won’t technically exist for another two months, but the two are already reportedly looking at substantial changes to the government’s status quo.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: Perplexity adds AI-assisted shopping features
item.description: Artificial intelligence search startup Perplexity on Monday launched a shopping hub to attract users, in a bid to beef up its platform as it attempts to take on Alphabet-owned Google’s dominance in the search engine space.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Trump watches as Musk's SpaceX launches Starship rocket
item.description: President-elect Trump joined Elon Musk on Tuesday in Texas to observe the launch of a SpaceX rocket as the two leaders have spent significant time together since Election Day. Trump was joined by some of his staff and Republican lawmakers, including Sens. Ted Cruz (R-Texas), Bill Hagerty (R-Tenn.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Amazon and SpaceX are challenging the NLRB’s constitutionality in federal court
item.description: Attorneys for Amazon and Elon Musk’s SpaceX argued in a federal appeals court Monday that the National Labor Relations Board’s structure is unconstitutional, advancing a legal fight that may last into the Trump administration where Musk is expected to oversee bureaucratic cost-cutting.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Overhaul of security clearance and personnel vetting process enters new phase
item.description: The Performance Accountability Council latest's report on “Trusted Workforce 2.0 said agencies have updated all major policies related to personnel vetting. The post Overhaul of security clearance and personnel vetting process enters new phase first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft extends Entra ID to WSL, WinGet
item.description: Microsoft has added new security features to Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL) and the Windows Package Manager (WinGet), including integration with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Active Directory) for identity-based access control.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: What to know about Howard Lutnick, Trump's pick for commerce secretary
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has chosen Howard Lutnick, head of brokerage and investment bank Cantor Fitzgerald and cryptocurrency enthusiast, as his nominee for commerce secretary.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Business Internet Security: Everything You Need to Consider
item.description: Cyber-attacks can cost companies millions of dollars in lost revenue, legal fees, and recovery efforts. A security breach can severely tarnish a company’s reputation and customer trust, making comprehensive internet security crucial for your small business.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Government commission proposes Manhattan Project-style AI funding plan
item.description: A U.S. congressional commission proposed a "Manhattan Project-like" initiative for the funding of artificial intelligence (AI) development in a larger push to stay ahead of China's technological advancements. The report, issued Tuesday by the bipartisan U.S.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Revolutionizing Interactive Experiences: The Unseen Heroes of AR and VR
item.description: Discover the innovative technologies shaping the future of augmented reality, virtual experiences, and interactivity.
item.dc:creator: Mark Wilson, Fast Company

item.title: These midsized companies bring fresh thinking to quietly important jobs
item.description: Equipment has always needed maintenance. We’ve been booking travel online for years. Even using speech recognition to create on-screen captions is not exactly a breakthrough new technology.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: The 9 next big things in consumer technology for 2024
item.description: If you want to get excited about consumer tech again, look at these innovations. They include breakthroughs in material design and miniaturization to make your gadgets even sleeker; new technologies to improve your health and wellness; and some clever applications of AI and data analysis.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: The 11 next big things in AI and data innovations for 2024
item.description: The AI big bang that first caught the world’s attention with ChatGPT is sending energy and innovation out in many directions. Large language models aren’t just chatting and summarizing text: Now they’re also reasoning through problems with increasing autonomy.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: The Department of Veterans Affairs' Approach to Automated Software Testing: A Model for Efficiency and Flexibility
item.description: Discover how the Department of Veterans Affairs uses open-source technology, a DevSecOps model, and AI/machine learning to streamline software testing processes.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft pitches AI 'agents' that can perform tasks on their own at Ignite 2024
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella told customers at a conference in Chicago on Tuesday that the company is teaching a new set of artificial intelligence tools how to "act on our behalf across our work and life.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: German police break up an environmental activists' camp near Tesla plant outside Berlin
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — German police broke up a protest camp housing environmental activists in a forest near the Tesla electric car factory in Grünheide outside Berlin on Tuesday, citing violations of public safety and order.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Succeeding with observability in the cloud
item.description: In its 2024 report, Splunk breaks down observability practices into four stages: foundational visibility, guided insights, proactive response, and unified workflows. Based on that, it classifies companies into four stages of observability maturity: beginning, emerging, evolving, and leading.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Akka distributed computing platform adds Java SDK
item.description: Akka, formerly known as Lightbend, has released Akka 3, an update to the JVM-based distributed computing platform that adds a Java SDK as well as serverless and “bring your own cloud” deployment options. Akka 3 and the company’s name change were announced November 15.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Lawrence Livermore supercomputer is crowned world’s speediest
item.description: "El Capitan" takes top spot in global supercomputer race, helping tackle daunting challenges in national security and science.
item.dc:creator: Lisa M. Krieger, The Mercury News

item.title: 1 in 5 get news from social media influencers: Pew poll
item.description: About 1 in 5 Americans say they get their news from social media influencers, according to a new poll from the Pew Research Center. Of the 21 percent of adults who said they get their news from influencers on social media, the split was roughly even across parties.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, The Hill

item.title: Company affiliated with Alex Jones seeks to disqualify The Onion's auction bid on Infowars
item.description: A federal bankruptcy judge in Texas is being asked to disqualify a winning bid made by the satirical news outlet The Onion to buy conspiracy theorist Alex Jones' Infowars.
item.dc:creator: Dave Collins, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The dirty little secret of open source contributions
item.description: “Nobody cares if you contribute.” That’s what a Postgres friend said to me during lunch at KubeCon when I suggested that hiring Postgres contributors could be a selling point for customers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI chatbot reportedly says to eat deadly mushrooms
item.description: “FungiFriend” was added to a popular mushroom discussion Facebook group and encouraged members to “sauté in butter” a potentially dangerous mushroom.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: The Onion CEO explains Infowars purchase amid Alex Jones's claims about auction
item.description: The Onion CEO Ben Collins confirmed that the publication purchased Alex Jones’s Infowars outlet in a Saturday series of posts on the social media platform Bluesky amid Jones's claims of wrongdoing and illegal activity.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Go language evolving for future hardware, AI workloads
item.description: The Go programming language having just turned 15 years old on November 10, proponents now are planning to adapt the Go language to large multicore systems, the latest vector and matrix hardware instructions, and the needs of AI workloads.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: In Cyprus, Ukrainians learn how to dispose of landmines that kill and maim hundreds
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — In a Cypriot National Guard camp, Ukrainians are being trained on how to identify, locate and dispose of landmines and other unexploded munitions that litter huge swaths of their country, killing and maiming hundreds of people, including children.
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: One Tech Tip: Replacing passwords with passkeys for an easier login experience
item.description: LONDON (AP) — If you're tired of memorizing passwords, then give passkeys a try. You might have noticed that many online services are now offering the option of using passkeys, a digital authentication method touted as an easier and more secure way to log in.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: How to use DispatchProxy for AOP in .NET Core
item.description: Aspect-oriented programming (AOP) decomposes an application’s source code into distinct aspects that isolate the core business logic from cross-cutting concerns.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Apple announces new feature to help airlines track your lost luggage
item.description: For air travelers who have popped an AirTag in their luggage – but still struggle to get help when suitcases are misplaced – this software update is for you.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: Chinese hackers target Tibetan websites in malware attack, cybersecurity group says
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A hacking group that is believed to be Chinese state-sponsored has compromised two websites with ties to the Tibetan community in an attack meant to install malware on users' computers, according to findings released Wednesday by a private cybersecurity firm.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
